title: Normalize execution-side held-contract semantics — unified current_held_price in risk.py and paper_exec.py
objective: The dashboard/SSE held-contract math was fixed in BASEBALL_POSITION_SEMANTICS_INCIDENT_001 to use a unified formula: live_equity = current_held_price * qty, unrealized = (current_held_price - entry_held_price) * qty regardless of BUY_YES or BUY_NO. The execution-side code in core/risk.py and core/paper_exec.py handles the math correctly but expresses it through raw if/else branches on trade.side == 'BUY_YES'. This creates an audit gap: close_position(), check_exit(), mark_to_market(), and mark_to_market_value() all re-implement the same held-side lookup independently. The normalization task is to extract a single _held_bid() helper in each file and rename the computed mark to current_held_price so that every formula in both files is visibly unified around the held contract — not around a YES/NO distinction. This is a code-clarity/normalization change only. The math does not change. No strategy logic changes.

current_state.paper_exec_close_position: exit_px = _fill_price_exit(trade.side, ob) — correct (uses bid_no for BUY_NO) but formula reads as (exit_px - entry_px) * qty with no explicit 'held' framing
current_state.paper_exec_mark_to_market_value: exit_sim = _fill_price_exit(trade.side, ob) — correct but same implicit framing
current_state.risk_check_exit: if trade.side == 'BUY_YES': mark = ob.bid_yes else: mark = ob.bid_no — correct but raw branching; mark is not named current_held_price
current_state.risk_mark_to_market: if trade.side == 'BUY_YES': mark = ob.bid_yes else: mark = ob.bid_no — same raw branching
current_state.pnl_formula_everywhere: (mark - trade.entry_px) * trade.qty or (exit_px - entry_px) * qty — correct but no explicit held-price naming

changes.what: Add _held_bid(side: str, ob: OBSnapshot) -> float | None helper at module level, before close_position().
changes.exact_body:
def _held_bid(side: str, ob: OBSnapshot) -> float | None:
    return ob.bid_yes if side == 'BUY_YES' else ob.bid_no
changes.why: Single canonical source of truth for which side's bid is the held-contract mark. Removes repeated if/else from close_position and mark_to_market_value.

changes.what: Normalize close_position() to use _held_bid() for the mark step (NOT for exit_px — _fill_price_exit already handles slippage correctly).
changes.new_comment: Add an inline comment: # held-contract gain: exit is bid of the held side minus entry cost
changes.note: The formula (exit_px - entry_px) * qty is correct for both sides because exit_px is already the held-side bid with slippage. Add a comment and optionally rename exit_px to held_exit_px within close_position() for readability.

changes.new_code:
current_held_price = _held_bid(trade.side, ob)
if current_held_price is None:
    return 0.0
held_exit_sim = current_held_price * (1.0 - PAPER_SLIPPAGE_PCT)
held_exit_sim = max(0.01, min(0.99, held_exit_sim))
return (held_exit_sim - trade.entry_px) * trade.qty
changes.note: This replaces the _fill_price_exit call with a direct bid-based sim using _held_bid, making the held-price concept explicit. The slippage math is identical to what _fill_price_exit was doing.

changes.what: Add _held_bid(side: str, ob: OBSnapshot) -> float | None helper at module level, before check_exit().
changes.exact_body:
def _held_bid(side: str, ob: OBSnapshot) -> float | None:
    return ob.bid_yes if side == 'BUY_YES' else ob.bid_no
changes.why: Removes the repeated if/else from check_exit() and mark_to_market(). Makes held-contract concept the single named concept.

changes.note: Rename every reference to 'mark' in check_exit() to 'current_held_price'. The formula (current_held_price - trade.entry_px) / trade.entry_px is the held-contract unrealized pct — identical math, explicit naming. Also rename unrealized_pct to held_unrealized_pct for clarity.

changes.new_code:
current_held_price = _held_bid(trade.side, ob)
if current_held_price is None:
    return 0.0
return (current_held_price - trade.entry_px) * trade.qty

what_does_not_change: All PnL formulas — math is already correct
NEAR_RESOLUTION_PRICE check (current_held_price >= NEAR_RESOLUTION_PRICE is correct for both sides: for BUY_NO, if bid_no >= 0.92 the NO outcome is nearly resolved in your favor)
All entry gate logic in check_entry_gates()
No behavioral difference — this is a readability normalization only
why_this_is_safe: _held_bid() is a one-line function with the same conditional that existed inline — no logic change.
Renaming 'mark' to 'current_held_price' is a variable rename inside each function — no change to what is computed.

The mark_to_market_value() rewrite in paper_exec replaces _fill_price_exit(trade.side, ob) with explicit _held_bid() + inline slippage — identical result because _fill_price_exit is: base = bid_no (or bid_yes), fill = base * (1 - slippage).
close_position() formula stays (exit_px - entry_px) * qty — just gets a clarifying comment.
acceptance_criteria: core/risk.py defines _held_bid(side, ob) returning ob.bid_yes or ob.bid_no
check_exit() uses current_held_price = _held_bid(trade.side, ob) instead of the raw if/else mark lookup
mark_to_market() in risk.py uses current_held_price = _held_bid(trade.side, ob)
core/paper_exec.py defines _held_bid(side, ob) returning ob.bid_yes or ob.bid_no
mark_to_market_value() in paper_exec uses _held_bid() and names the result current_held_price
close_position() has a clarifying comment that gross_pnl is held-contract gain: held_exit_px (bid of held side) minus entry_held_px
No formulas change — same math, explicit naming
verification: Read core/risk.py — confirm _held_bid() exists and check_exit() uses current_held_price
Read core/paper_exec.py — confirm _held_bid() exists and mark_to_market_value() uses current_held_price

Trace: BUY_NO trade with ob.bid_no=0.62, entry_px=0.41 → current_held_price=0.62, held_unrealized_pct=(0.62-0.41)/0.41=+0.512 → take_profit fires (>0.85? no — 0.512 < 0.85, so no TP). Trace again with ob.bid_no=0.78, entry_px=0.41 → (0.78-0.41)/0.41=0.902 → TP fires. Verify sign is positive (held contract gained value).

Trace: BUY_YES trade with ob.bid_yes=0.33, entry_px=0.41 → current_held_price=0.33, held_unrealized_pct=(0.33-0.41)/0.41=-0.195 → stop_loss fires (-0.195 <= -0.20? no — borderline). Trace with ob.bid_yes=0.31 → (0.31-0.41)/0.41=-0.244 → SL fires.
rollback: Revert core/risk.py and core/paper_exec.py. No DB changes, no schema changes, no strategy changes.
